steps.title: Parallel lines have equal slopes — read slope directly from standard form
steps.content:
For $ax + by = c$ the slope is $-a/b$, no algebra needed.
Line $j$: $5x + 6y = 66$ $\Rightarrow$ slope $= -5/6$.
Line $k$: $20x + ry = 18$ $\Rightarrow$ slope $= -20/r$.
Set equal: $-5/6 = -20/r$ $\Rightarrow$ $r = 24$.

steps.title: Plug the y-intercept point into line k
steps.content:
Line $k$ is $20x + 24y = 18$. The point $(0, b)$ lies on it, so substitute $x = 0$:
$$24b = 18 \implies b = \frac{3}{4}$$
The answer is $\boxed{0.75}$ (or $3/4$).
